intro x y
      have hx : ∃ (x' : R), x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x' := by sorry
      have hy : ∃ (y' : R), y = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) y' := by sorry

        _ = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by sorry

intro x y
      have h : ∀ (x y : R ⧸ ker f),
        ((kerLift f) (x * y) : S) = ((kerLift f) x : S) * ((kerLift f) y : S) := by
        intro x y
        -- We need to show that the kernel lift preserves multiplication
        -- Since `kerLift f` is a ring homomorphism, this should follow from its definition
        -- Specifically, `kerLift f` is constructed as `Ideal.Quotient.lift`, which respects multiplication
        -- We can use the fact that `Ideal.Quotient.lift` is a ring homomorphism
        rw [show (kerLift f) (x * y) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (x * y) by rfl]
        rw [show (kerLift f) x = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f x by rfl]
        rw [show (kerLift f) y = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f y by rfl]
        -- Now we use the fact that `Ideal.Quotient.lift` is a ring homomorphism to conclude
        have h₁ : Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (x * y) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f x * Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f y := by
          apply Ideal.Quotient.map_mul_preimage
        rw [h₁]
      -- Now we apply the general statement to the specific x and y
      exact h x y
  }

intro x y

          try simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
          <;>
          try aesop
        exact h2
      exact h1
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = f (x.val * y.val) := by
        have h₂ : Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x.val * y.val) = x * y := by
          rw [← Ideal.Quotient.eq]
          <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.mk, RingHom.map_mul]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker, RingHom.map_mul])
          <;>
          (try ring_nf at *)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker, RingHom.map_mul])
          <;>
          (try aesop)
        calc
          kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x.val * y.val)) := by rw [h₂]
          _ = f (x.val * y.val) := by
            rw [kerLift_mk]
            <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.mk]
      have h₂ : kerLift f x * kerLift f y = f x.val * f y.val := by
        calc
          kerLift f x * kerLift f y = (kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x.val)) * (kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) y.val)) := by
            (try simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk])
          _ = f x.val * f y.val := by
            simp [kerLift_mk, Ideal.Quotient.mk]
          _ = f x.val * f y.val := by rfl
      calc
        kerLift f (x * y) = f (x.val * y.val) := by rw [h₁]
        _ = f x.val * f y.val := by
          apply f.map_mul
        _ = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
          rw [h₂]
  }

have h₁ : ∀ (x y : R ⧸ ker f), kerLift f (x * y) = (kerLift f x) * (kerLift f y) := by
        intro x y
        rw [show kerLift f (x * y) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x * y)) by rfl]
        have h₂ : f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x * y)) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x * Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) y) := by
          congr 1 <;>
            simp [Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff, mul_add, add_mul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
          <;>
            ring_nf
          <;>
            aesop
        rw [h₂]
        have h₃ : f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x * Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) y) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) x) * f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) y) := by
          exact map_mul f _ _
        rw [h₃]
        <;> simp [kerLift_mk]
        <;>
          aesop
      intro x y
      apply h₁
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = f (x.val * y.val) := by
        rw [kerLift_mk]
        <;> simp [Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      have h₂ : kerLift f x * kerLift f y = f (x.val) * f (y.val) := by
        rw [kerLift_mk, kerLift_mk]
      have h₃ : f (x.val * y.val) = f (x.val) * f (y.val) := by
        rw [map_mul]
      rw [h₁, h₂, h₃]
  }

have h : ∀ (x y : R ⧸ ker f), (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) (x * y) = (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) x * (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) y := by
        intro x y

-- Use the fact that kerLift is a ring homomorphism to preserve multiplication
      have h : ∀ (x y : R ⧸ ker f), kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
        intro x y
        -- Since kerLift f is defined as a lift of the ring homomorphism f, it preserves multiplication
        -- This is a property of the lift construction
        have h₁ : kerLift f = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (by
          intro a ha
          simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]
          ) := rfl
        rw [h₁]
        -- Use the property of the lift function to preserve multiplication
        apply Ideal.Quotient.map_mul'
      -- Apply the above result to complete the proof
      exact h
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = f (x * y) := by
        simp [kerLift_mk]
        <;>
        simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
        <;>
        aesop

      have h₂ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f y) = (f x * f y : S) := by
        have h₃ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x = f x := by
          simp [kerLift_mk]
          <;>
          simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
          <;>
          aesop
        have h₄ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y = f y := by
          simp [kerLift_mk]
          <;>
          simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
          <;>
          aesop
        rw [h₃, h₄]
        <;>
        simp [RingHom.map_mul]

      have h₃ : f (x * y) = f x * f y := by
        have h₄ : (f : R →+* S) (x * y) = (f : R →+* S) x * (f : R →+* S) y := by
          apply RingHom.map_mul
        simpa using h₄

      have h₄ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y := by
        calc
          (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = f (x * y) := by rw [h₁]
          _ = f x * f y := by rw [h₃]
          _ = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y := by
            have h₅ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x = f x := by
              simp [kerLift_mk]
              <;>
              simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
              <;>
              aesop
            have h₆ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y = f y := by
              simp [kerLift_mk]
              <;>
              simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.mk_eq_mk_iff_sub_mem]
              <;>
              aesop
            rw [h₅, h₆]
            <;>
            simp [RingHom.map_mul]

      exact h₄
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (Ideal.Quotient.liftAux (ker f) (f) _) * Ideal.Quotient.liftAux (ker f) (f) _) := by rfl
      have h₂ : kerLift f x = f (Ideal.Quotient.liftAux (ker f) (f) _) := by rfl
      have h₃ : kerLift f y = f (Ideal.Quotient.liftAux (ker f) (f) _) := by rfl
      simp_all [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift, Ideal.Quotient.liftAux]

      (try simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]) <;>

      <;>
      (try simp_all [RingHom.map_mul])
      <;>
